hypothesis: Contrastive alignment between SMILES and 3D electron density grids will induce emergent invariance not only to global SO(3) rotations but also to local conformational changes, such that a model pretrained exclusively on single low-energy conformers will retrieve alternative conformers of the same molecule (differing by rotatable bond torsions) with significantly higher Recall@10 than a unimodal 3D baseline, because the invariant SMILES anchor forces the volumetric encoder to suppress conformer-specific spatial features in favor of topology-preserving chemical features—and this conformer-invariance will be quantitatively stronger for rigid aromatic scaffolds than for flexible aliphatic chains, providing a falsifiable prediction testable by constructing a benchmark of molecules with systematically varied torsional states generated via RDKit conformer sampling.
